start.speaker: The Overseer
start.text: The new arithmetic. I know what you did at the gate, in the camp, and by the east posts. The Vale reports everything to me eventually. Say what you came to say.

options.text: I know what the barrier eats.

measure.speaker: The Overseer
measure.text: Everyone does, once. Then the Vale takes theirs. Come back when you have something worth the candle — or when the chained man's tongue loosens yours.

confrontation.speaker: The Overseer
confrontation.text: So Essek talked. Yes — the barrier is fed. Nine hundred souls keep forty thousand safe from what sleeps under this valley. I keep the tally so no one else has to. Now you know the price of the wall. What will you do about it — with what you've made of yourself?

options.text: [Might 3] Walk past him and shatter the tally-pylon with your bare hands.

effects.text: The pylon cracked like river ice. Whatever sleeps below is waking, and every soul in the Vale is finally free to face it. You chose the honest catastrophe.

options.text: [Guile 3] The tally has errors. You know, because you put them there. Propose a quiet partnership.

effects.text: The Overseer keeps the wall. You keep the Overseer. Nobody outside this room will ever know who runs the Vale now.

options.text: [Lore 3] The feeding rite is a translation error. The old rune means 'witness', not 'consume'. Show him.

effects.text: Four centuries of feeding, from one mistranslated rune. The barrier only ever needed to be watched. The Overseer sat down in the ash and wept.

options.text: [Heart 3] The nine hundred deserve to choose. Call the camp together and tell them everything.

effects.text: You told the camp the truth under the barrier's glow. Some raged. Some volunteered. All of them chose. The Vale is still a cage — but its people are no longer cattle.

options.text: I... need to think about this.

ending_shattered.speaker: The Overseer
ending_shattered.text: Then it's done. Four hundred years of arithmetic, spent in a fistfall. Run if you like. It won't matter, and that's the freest any of us has ever been.

ending_partnership.speaker: The Overseer
ending_partnership.text: You'd have made a fine Crown auditor. Very well — partner. The wall stands, the tally lies, and the Vale sleeps another century. Wash your hands on the way out.

ending_rewritten.speaker: The Overseer
ending_rewritten.text: 'Witness.' All this time. All those names in my tally, and it only wanted... someone to watch. Take the keeper's seat, scholar. I have four centuries of apologies to begin.

ending_chosen.speaker: The Overseer
ending_chosen.text: Nine hundred voices instead of one tally. It will be slower. Crueller, some years. But it will be theirs. I find I can live with being unnecessary.
